Politics.headline: Trump Releasing 172 Million Barrels of Oil from Reserve as Prices Soar Amid Iran War
Politics.detail: President Trump ordered the release of 172 million barrels of oil from the U.S.'s Strategic Petroleum Reserve on Wednesday, after oil prices rocketed to their highest levels in years amid the U.S.'s war with Iran.

Politics.headline: Pentagon Blocks Photographers from Hegseth's Briefings on the Iran War
Politics.detail: The Pentagon is reportedly preventing press photographers from attending Congressman Hegseth's briefings regarding the Iran war. The reasons cited are 'unflattering' photos, raising concerns about media access.

Politics.headline: Chile's New President to Signal Swift Alignment with Trump
Politics.detail: Chile's newly inaugurated right-wing president, Jose Antonio Kast, will quickly align with the Trump administration, planning to sign agreements with the U.S. to boost cooperation on critical minerals and security matters. Kast was sworn in on Wednesday, replacing leftist Gabriel Boric.

Politics.headline: China is rebuilding its grip on North Korea. Is Kim Jong Un ready to oblige?
Politics.detail: China is working to reassert its influence over North Korea, which has grown closer to Russia since the Ukraine invasion, with Kim Jong Un and Chinese Premier Li Qiang engaging in high-level talks to boost trade and investment. North Korea has been supplying troops and weapons to Moscow in exchange for fuel and food, due to UN sanctions over its nuclear weapons program.

Politics.headline: Taiwan's Cabinet Addresses Potential Economic and Defense Impact of Iran War
Politics.detail: Taiwan has implemented emergency price controls on gas and diesel because the war in the Middle East is driving up oil prices, and the conflict is not expected to affect Taiwan's purchases of drones from the US. Taiwan's premier stated that there have been no delays to drone purchases.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Futures Fall as Oil Surges Again
Finance & Economy.detail: US stock futures fell sharply on Thursday as oil prices resumed their advance, fueling concerns about resurgent inflation. WTI crude climbed back above $90 a barrel as the prospect of a prolonged Iran war overshadowed a coordinated release of oil reserves by major economies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global airlines hike ticket prices as Iran war sends costs soaring
Finance & Economy.detail: The war has sent oil prices surging, upending global travel and pushing airline ticket costs on some routes sky-high.

Finance & Economy.headline: US consumers express dismay over rising gas prices after attack on Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices have shot up as the war snarls transportation and production in one of the most energy-rich regions on earth.

Finance & Economy.headline: India to release product list for faster PN3 FDI approvals.
Finance & Economy.detail: FDI policy eased: Relaxed PN3 opens door for global funds with Chinese stakes.

Finance & Economy.headline: Shell declares force majeure on LNG contracts from Qatar
Finance & Economy.detail: Qatar announced a production halt at a facility last week and declared force majeure on LNG shipments.

Technology.headline: GFiber Is Being Absorbed Into Astound Broadband
Technology.detail: Google parent Alphabet is only maintaining a minority stake into the new entity, with Astound's parent, the investment firm Stonepeak, holding the majority.

Technology.headline: Microsoft and retired military chiefs back AI company Anthropic in court fight against Pentagon
Technology.detail: Microsoft and a group of retired military leaders are backing Anthropic in court to block the Trump administration from labeling the AI company a supply chain risk.

Technology.headline: Microsoft urges major changes to Washington data center regulations as bill nears final vote
Technology.detail: Microsoft is advocating for significant alterations to data center regulations in Washington as a crucial bill approaches its final vote.

Technology.headline: TikTok to Let Apple Music Users Stream Full Songs Without Ever Leaving the App
Technology.detail: TikTok is introducing a feature that allows Apple Music subscribers to listen to full songs within the TikTok app.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating the pace and scale of cyberattacks.

Science.headline: Overlooked brainstem pathway controls human hands
Science.detail: Researchers have identified a network of connections linking the brainstem and spinal cord that helps control hand and arm movements, revealing an unexpected layer of the nervous system enabling people to grasp, hold, and manipulate objects. By mapping this pathway, researchers say the work could help guide new therapies aimed at restoring hand and arm function after stroke or other neurological injuries.

Science.headline: Why low-frequency spectrum is emerging as a quiet contender for future networks
Science.detail: Low-frequency wireless networks are emerging as a powerful new tool for regional industries, offering long-range, reliable connectivity that keeps sensors, cameras and critical systems online across Australia's vast landscapes. This technology can help deliver the science Australians need for a productive, sustainable, healthy and secure nation.

Science.headline: Science parks' combined revenue hits record NT$5.8tn
Science.detail: The nation's three major science parks last year posted a combined revenue of NT$5.8 trillion (US$182.6 billion), up 21.83 percent year-on-year and a new record, according to the National Science and Technology Council. The strong performance was attributed to continued growth in the semiconductor industry, driven by strong demand for AI chips and emerging technologies.

Science.headline: Scientists Observe Electrical Discharges on Trees under Thunderstorms
Science.detail: Scientists have recently observed electrical discharges on trees during thunderstorms. This phenomenon sheds light on the complex interactions between atmospheric electricity and terrestrial ecosystems.

Science.headline: Extremophile Bacteria May Hitch Rides on Asteroid Fragments
Science.detail: New research suggests that extremophile bacteria could survive asteroid impacts strong enough to launch them into space. This indicates that life could potentially travel between planetary bodies via asteroid fragments.

Health.headline: Scientists May Have Found a Pill for Sleep Apnea
Health.detail: A European clinical trial found that the drug sulthiame significantly reduced breathing interruptions in people with moderate to severe sleep apnea, with patients taking higher doses experiencing up to 47% fewer pauses in breathing and improved oxygen levels.

Health.headline: Cannabis Study Finds THC Can Create False Memories
Health.detail: A controlled experiment showed that cannabis users were more likely to recall words that were never shown and struggled with tasks like remembering to do something later, suggesting THC can create new memories that never happened.

Health.headline: Combining exercise and sleep coaching can help improve sleep and health markers in young women.
Health.detail: Combining exercise and sleep coaching can help improve sleep and health markers in young women.

Health.headline: Large-scale study challenges link between acetaminophen use during pregnancy and autism risk in children.
Health.detail: A recent large-scale study challenges the previously suggested link between acetaminophen use during pregnancy and an increased risk of autism in children.

Health.headline: Global Fund Faces $5bn Shortfall As France Slashes Support, EU Delays Pledge
Health.detail: The Global Fund's eighth replenishment secured $12.64 billion against its $18 billion target to fight AIDS, tuberculosis, and malaria, facing a significant shortfall due to cuts from France, a delayed EU pledge, and a US pullback. Reaching the full target would have prevented roughly 400 million new infections between 2027 and 2029.

Sports.headline: Real win in Champions League last 16; Arsenal held
Sports.detail: Federico Valverde scored a hat-trick as Real Madrid defeated Manchester City 3-0, and Arsenal secured a 1-1 draw against Bayer Leverkusen in the Champions League last-16 first leg. Valverde's performance and Havertz's late penalty for Arsenal were key moments in the matches.

Sports.headline: Real Madrid stun Man City 3-0 as PSG hit Chelsea for five in Champions League
Sports.detail: Real Madrid's Federico Valverde scored a hat-trick in a 3-0 victory over Manchester City, while Paris Saint-Germain defeated Chelsea 5-2 in the Champions League. Other notable results included Arsenal's 1-1 draw with Bayer Leverkusen and Bodo/Glimt's 3-0 win against Sporting.

Sports.headline: Fans, minister welcome national baseball team
Sports.detail: Taiwan's World Baseball Classic team was welcomed by the Minister of Sports and over 300 fans upon their return from Japan, despite not advancing to the quarter-finals. The team tied for second place in Pool C but lost the tiebreaker to South Korea.

Sports.headline: Soccer-US lawmakers urge FIFA to lower 2026 World Cup ticket prices
Sports.detail: U.S. lawmakers are urging FIFA to lower ticket prices for the 2026 World Cup, criticizing the use of dynamic pricing which they say makes the event exclusionary. They have asked FIFA to redistribute unallocated tickets at more affordable prices and reconsider dynamic pricing.

Sports.headline: Rouyn-Noranda vs Rimouski is postponed
Sports.detail: Thegame between the Rouyn-Noranda Huskies and the Rimouski Océanic, scheduled for Wednesday night, has been postponed due to weather conditions. Further details regarding rescheduling will be announced later.

Entertainment.headline: Suspect in Rihanna shooting now charged with attempted murder
Entertainment.detail: A woman who allegedly fired shots at Rihanna's house in Beverly Hills is now charged with attempted murder. Rihanna, A$AP Rocky, and their three children were home at the time of the incident.

Entertainment.headline: 'The King's Warden' hits 12 million admissions, becomes Korea's 20th most-watched film of all time
Entertainment.detail: The Korean historical drama 'The King's Warden' has surpassed 12 million moviegoers, making it the 20th most-watched film in Korean history. The film exceeded the 11.91 million admissions recorded by 'Exhuma'.

Entertainment.headline: Winnie the Pooh is turning 100
Entertainment.detail: Winnie the Pooh, created by A.A. Milne, is celebrating its 100th anniversary. Disney, which acquired the rights in the 1960s, is holding a year-long celebration.

Entertainment.headline: Selena Gomez, Benny Blanco's 'ideal' baby timeline revealed amid surrogacy rumours
Entertainment.detail: Amid surrogacy rumors, details of Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco's ideal baby timeline have been revealed. Stay updated with the latest celebrity news and entertainment updates.

Entertainment.headline: Shakti Mohan reveals why she refused to be paid for choreographing Padmaavat's 'Nainowale Ne'
Entertainment.detail: Shakti Mohan disclosed her reasons for declining payment for choreographing 'Nainowale Ne' in Padmaavat, mentioning that people 'hold their breath' around Sanjay Leela Bhansali.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ecuador prepares for attack on 'criminal economy' with Trump backing
Crime & Justice.detail: Interior Minister John Reimberg warned residents in four provinces to obey a curfew to avoid 'collateral damage'.

Crime & Justice.headline: L.A. County deputy collected paychecks after felony excessive force conviction
Crime & Justice.detail: A Los Angeles County deputy continued to collect paychecks even after a felony excessive force conviction.

Crime & Justice.headline: $1-million reward offered for California murder suspect, an FBI 'Ten Most Wanted' fugitive
Crime & Justice.detail: A $1-million reward is being offered for Omar Alexander Cardenas in a fatal Sylmar shooting, who is also one of the FBI's “Ten Most Wanted” fugitives.

Crime & Justice.headline: South African soldiers deploy in Johannesburg to tackle crime and gangs
Crime & Justice.detail: South African National Defence Force (SANDF) soldiers are patrolling as part of efforts to tackle crime and gangs.

Crime & Justice.headline: US government contractor accused of stealing $46M arrested on small island, FBI announces
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI and French authorities arrested a former government contractor accused of stealing $46 million in cryptocurrency from the U.S. Marshals Service.

Education.headline: Primary school in Kent to receive £1.2 million in funding for expansion plans
Education.detail: A primary school in Kent will receive £1.2 million in funding to build three new classrooms at Ebbsfleet Green Primary School, driven by rapid growth in the area and delays to the opening of a new primary provision elsewhere. The investment aims to maintain high standards for current pupils and open up vital new early years opportunities to meet the growing needs in the local area.

Education.headline: Transforming higher education: a global roadmap for the future
Education.detail: UNESCO's new publication, "Transforming higher education: Global collaboration on visioning and action", provides a roadmap to reimagine the sector and highlight priority areas for action, as global higher education expands rapidly. The publication recognizes that higher education must evolve to realize its transformative potential and contribute to sustainable and inclusive development.

Education.headline: Luxembourg's only university embroiled in row over governance
Education.detail: Luxembourg's only university is currently facing a governance dispute. Details surrounding the row are still emerging.

Education.headline: ‘Near universal’ student AI use leading to ‘polarised views’
Education.detail: A recent report indicates that the near-universal use of AI by students is resulting in polarized viewpoints. Further details about the nature of these views are yet to be disclosed.

Education.headline: With professors missing, medical students turn to YouTube and coaching apps to fix learning gaps
Education.detail: Due to missing professors, medical students are increasingly relying on resources like YouTube and coaching applications to address gaps in their learning. This trend highlights the challenges in medical education and the adaptive measures students are taking to ensure their understanding of the material.

Lifestyle.headline: Women reveal surprising effects of being bald: 'It's a luxury'
Lifestyle.detail: Several women share their experiences and insights on the unexpected benefits and freedoms that come with choosing to be bald.

Lifestyle.headline: Researchers find TikTok fitness videos cause body image issues
Lifestyle.detail: A study reveals a correlation between viewing fitness content on TikTok and increased body image concerns among users.

Lifestyle.headline: Reindeer racing thrills spectators in Finland's frigid north near the Russian border
Lifestyle.detail: The traditional Reindeer Cup in Salla, Finland, drew hundreds of fans to watch the exciting races.

Lifestyle.headline: Art, pantun, kueh and community: The Peranakan Museum hosts its 10th Armenian Street Party
Lifestyle.detail: The Peranakan Museum's Armenian Street Party will feature art installations, cooking demonstrations, food pop-ups and a mystery-themed experience based on the drama Aunty Lee's Deadly Delights from March 13 to 15.

Lifestyle.headline: NHS doctor shares 5 'normal' symptoms women should seek urgent medical help for
Lifestyle.detail: An NHS doctor highlights five seemingly ordinary symptoms that women should not ignore and promptly seek medical attention for.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDSA People Power: One true thing in recent PH history
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Yen Makabenta reflects on the significance of EDSA People Power in Philippine history, published on March 12, 2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Will the Iran war destroy the American empire?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Ricardo Saludo discusses the potential impact of the Iran war on the American empire. The article was published on March 12, 2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A sacred honor: Gratitude to the Egyptian president for the Bibliotheca Alexandrina International Prize
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Glenn S. Banaguas expresses gratitude to the Egyptian president for the Bibliotheca Alexandrina International Prize in an article published March 12, 2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Ad Hoc Absurdity: Why Commissions Fail While 'Ghosts' Prosper
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discusses the failures of commissions and the prosperity of 'ghosts'. The article was published on March 12, 2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Yellow Envelope Act: 'World Changed, Real Boss Step Forward'
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the implementation of the Yellow Envelope Act and its potential impact on labor relations, highlighting concerns about excessive actions by subcontract unions.  It was published on March 12, 2026.

World.headline: UN Security Council demands Iran halt attacks on Gulf nations
World.detail: The UN Security Council has called for Iran to cease its attacks on Gulf nations amidst escalating tensions in the region. The demand comes as the conflict continues to disrupt international shipping and energy markets.

World.headline: US responsible for deadly missile strike on Iran school, preliminary inquiry says
World.detail: A preliminary inquiry has determined that a deadly missile strike on an Iranian school was the responsibility of the U.S., sparking further outrage and condemnation. The incident has further strained relations between the two countries.

World.headline: Iran sets three conditions for peace; oil tankers in Iraqi port attacked
World.detail: Iran has announced three conditions for establishing peace, while attacks on oil tankers in an Iraqi port have added to the ongoing tensions in the region. The attacks have further destabilized the already volatile situation.

World.headline: Chile's new president to signal swift alignment with Trump
World.detail: Chile's newly inaugurated right-wing president, Jose Antonio Kast, is expected to quickly align the country with the policies of U.S. President Donald Trump. Agreements are planned to boost cooperation on critical minerals and security matters.

World.headline: Airlines around world hike fares as Iran war sparks global fuel price surge
World.detail: Airlines worldwide are increasing fares in response to surging global fuel prices caused by the ongoing war with Iran. The conflict's impact on fuel costs is rippling through the travel industry, affecting consumers globally.

Zimbabwe.headline: Kadoma court delays Patterson Timba rape case, key state witnesses already heard
Zimbabwe.detail: The Kadoma court has delayed the Patterson Timba rape case after key state witnesses were already heard.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mugabe's son and cousin seek deal to plead guilty in attempted murder case
Zimbabwe.detail: Robert Mugabe's son and cousin are seeking a deal to plead guilty in an attempted murder case.

Zimbabwe.headline: Sheriff cleared to seize Mahere's property to recover US$33k in Machakaire lawsuit
Zimbabwe.detail: The Sheriff has been cleared to seize Fadzai Mahere's property to recover US$33,000 in the Machakaire lawsuit.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bulawayo engages Chiwenga over illegal riverbed mining
Zimbabwe.detail: Bulawayo is engaging Chiwenga over illegal riverbed mining.

Zimbabwe.headline: US rejects claims it sought Zimbabwe's minerals in $367m health-aid talks
Zimbabwe.detail: The United States has rejected claims that it sought Zimbabwe's minerals in $367m health-aid talks.
